I like to have my SSID broadcast disabled, for increased security. Sadly, this device will not work if SSID broadcast is disabled. It would be nice to be able to manually enter the SSID (like every other device I have with WiFi, e.g., my phone, my desktop, my laptop, my tablet...). So, every time I have a power outage to my AirTies (admittedly not a common occurrence), I have to turn on SSID broadcast (and WPA for some reason!) on my router, let AirTies discover it, and then I can turn off SSID broadcast again. NOTE: I have an ATT branded AirTies.

I bought my PS3 over 4 years ago. A 40GB model that I've never once cleaned, not realizing that that's something that needed to be done. Well, a few weeks ago, my PS3 was having trouble reading games, but not anything else. And the discs rotated while in the Playstation, so I knew it wasn't a laser issue. And then it stopped reading the games altogether. So, we ordered a lens cleaner with "wind technology" and that did not work at all. Not even after 10 times of running it. My dad and I ended up taking some of the Playstation apart and cleaning it that way, but we couldn't get to the lens to clean it and we didn't want to risk breaking it. So, I then ordered this product with the brushes and stuff. After about 10-15 times running it through, my system reads the games with no problem, as if it were brand new! It took so many times running it through its cleaning process because of 4+ years of smoke(from my dad), dust, and whatever else could get in there that just built up until I could no longer play games. I will be cleaning my PS3 monthly with this product(although after awhile you'll need to get a new one). My dad doesn't smoke anymore, so that should help. I recommend not smoking around yours or anyone elses Playstation. I absolutely recommend this product. You need to get this. You will NOT be disappointed.

I received one of these IEIK Nano V3.0 controllers as a Christmas present. It works very well. I liked this version of the Nano because it plugs into the typical proto-board. I like this product so much that, I ordered four more for future projects. Please note: I do not use the Arduino IDE and so, I can't speak as to how well this controller performs with the Arduino IDE. I use the ImageCraft ICCAVR C compiler for writing my programs and Atmel's AVRStudio for downloading the respective code into the AVR microcontroller. This controller works flawlessly with the development tools that I do use. In addition, I remove the 16.000MHz crystal and replace it with a 18.432MHz crystal so I have BAUD rate friendly USART selections. In doing this, I sacrifice any convenience that the Arduino IDE offers. But then too, I have complete freedom over every detail of the AVR controller and code being developed. I know this brand of Arduino clone works with the development tools that I prefer to use and, I'll be recommending it and using it for as long as it does.

EDIT: A year after purchase, every cable I've used from this set has stopped working. There's no visible physical damage to these cables - they just gave up. I only use these cables to charge my devices and they simply do not charge where other cables work just fine. Instead of trying again I went with a different brand. Very disappointing for what seemed like such a high-quality product. ORIGINAL: A quality USB cable might not seem important but for someone who uses their electronics on a daily basis, a broken USB cable can be a major inconvenience. I had FOUR micro USB cables purchased from various stores that were ALL broken in some way & I was on the hunt to find cables that were durable and long-lasting. I've only owned these Volutz cables for a few days but I couldn't be more impressed. These are the OtterBoxes/Lifeproofs of cables - they do NOT bend at the spot beyond the plug where every other cable I owned fell apart. I can't talk about data transfer quality but the charging speed of these cables is excellent and there's no crazy overheating that can occur with low-quality products. I've already recommended these cables to friends and would buy these again!

I obtained a pouch of the Spark LCD cleaning wipes through Amazon's Vine program. According to Amazon's 5-star rating system, 3 stars means "it's okay," which is exactly how I'd rate this product. The main issue here is that the wipes are so wet, and apparently so water based, that they leave marks when the liquid dries. This is not a big deal when you use them, say, on a laptop screen if you're primarily use your laptop in doors in indoor lighting. The streaks on an LCD screen in a car, though, are very annoying. You can dry the screen with a microfiber cloth, but that begs the question, why not just use a microfiber cloth for the cleaning and drying in the first place? So, I'd only recommend these in lighting conditions in which the streaks are not going to be a problem.

I was recently involved in a car crash. This enclosure was in a little rubber case, inside my computer bag. Upon eventually connecting the drive, it starting making some strange sounds. Low and behold, my drive was damaged and it had all my footage from NYC on it. I am now stuck with a $2500 bill from a data recovery company because they said that the enclosure was not enough to protect this drive from shock. My drive is awaiting repair and has sustained severe platter damage. Stay STAY CLEAR OF THIS CASE!!!! I suggest ordering something that has much more shock protection in case it is dropped or hit. I feel like reaching out to this company to demand that they pay for this! This enclosure is also supposed to protect it! Yet here I am now paying $2500 for a recovery thanks to this piece of junk. I had other drives in my bag which have been checked also and found that there is no functional error with the other ones that were NOT in cases but also in my bag, so I DARE them to try and say it wasn't their product.

For the money, this is a great security system. Pros 1. Easy to set up (lots of reviews saying that the instructions are bad, difficult to configure, et). This could not be further from the truth. The instructions are descent, but if you have any kind of technical mind, this is as simple as it gets. * Run ethernet in your attic or however you choose. (this is POE and is your camera's POWER and DATA) *find a spot for your DVR and monitor (need your own monitor, can be HDMI or VGA) *Drill a couple of holes for wires, mount your cameras *Plug power, ethernet POE from cameras and monitor to the box *run an ethernet cord from box to your router/internet source *It turns on, starts recording and is ready to roll *login with admin and no password *download XMEye to your phone or go to www.xmeye.net., register an account and hardward, put the serial # in from the DVR box or scan the barcode with your phone and your can view your camera's remotely. *adjust camera angles using remote view on your phone (you watch on your phone, walk in front of the camera and there is NO delay at all. It is real time. Cameras seem high quality Video quality is very good Cons I didn't spring for panning cameras Otherwise, this is a great system for less than $300.

At least a couple buyers mention problems when plugging in high wattage items (teapot, small A/C unit, ...). To prevent electrical related fires high watt items (fridges, space heaters, window A/C's, some power tools, etc...) must be plugged directly into wall outlets. Drawing such power through most extension cords, power strips, and such, will overheat their wiring unless the cords are 12 gauge. Most extension cords, power strips, and such are at best 14 or 16 gauge. Bigger gauge numbers mean small wire diameters. Bigger wire diameters take bigger wattage, smaller diameters limit safe wattage. Loose receptacles (plug-in points) may also result in overheating and or electrical fires. Loose connections allow for electrical arcing (sparking) as well as poor electrical flow.

I purchased this unit after a careful evaluation and comparisons to numerous other comparable ones. I started using these headphones at the beginning of August. Initially, I was satisfied overall with the sound quality, and fit and finish. I jogged several times with these (30-90 minutes each time) and the ear buds stayed in place. The ear pieces would be covered in sweat and moisture, but they wiped off easily and seemed to work fine the next time. By October, however, I noticed that the charge didn't hold as long as the first time (Per other reviewers, this seemed to be a recurring theme). Even when I had the unit turned off, the battery would continue to drain and I had to recharge each time. Now it's November, and the right ear piece does not work at all, as in there is no sound emitted. Basically, this set of headphones lasted about 3 1/2 months of relatively low usage and responsible care. It's now beyond the return window and will just sit in a corner of my room collecting dust. I can't recommend this product, as I'm thoroughly disappointed in quality and durability. Stay away from these.

This chip is basically pushing the limits of that architecture (skylake refresh). And be prepared to either get a beefy air cooler (Noctua U12s, D15, be quiet Dark Rock Pro 4) or a 360mm AiO or go full custom water cooling because this chip needs all the cooling it can get. It's HOT. VERY VERY hot. I have mine delidded (yes I delidded because the Intel STIM can't do anything to cool this CPU), and run it bare with no IHS with a direct die frame, and it's still very hot. Despite all the negative things I've said. This is a very fast chip, and if you have the money... 100% go for it. Especially if budget isn't an issue, and you want the best. Just seeing all 8 cores and 16 threads at 5.0ghz is a sight of beauty. Best part about this chip is, if you're a streamer you can choose between NVENC or x264. I'm able to run x264 in OBS on Fast preset, and I don't notice any hiccups. This chip is a champ. Hopefully Intel can bring us a little more next year, as it seems like they wont be releasing any mainstream desktop CPU's in 2019, and the only thing we'll be getting is a 9900KS (factory binned and overclocked at 5.0ghz on all 8 cores).

I ended up updating the Firmware, to Version 1.0.11.128_10.2.112, about 3 days ago, and my various WiFi devices have connected to the Internet without issues since then. I will try to update this review if problems return. I'm disappointed that Netgear put out a version of firmware that wasn't stable for me, but hoping the updated firmware stays stable. Also disappointed that the router didn't update its firmware automatically since it appeared to be set to do that. ***Update Nov. 16, 2021 I had just one more "lock-up" incident with this router, on Nov. 9, but no more lock-ups in the past week, so I'm feeling somewhat more positive about this router. Beware of the Asus RX 580 Dual 4G OC card. On the surface, a sub $200 RX 580 is an incredible deal but there is a reason why it's so inexpensive. The memory modules are not cooled (see pictures) and this is completely unacceptable especially when considering that the RX 500 series are pretty much overclocked RX 400 cards with a bios flash. Those memory chips will eventually burn up and render the card useless. No wonder there are so many reviews here on Amazon and on Newegg mentioning dead cards. Asus, this is unacceptable. Your engineering team could have least included a bracket for this card to cool the chips around the processor. What were your thinking? Trying to save a couple of bucks?

I have 2 pairs of these just for the convenience of having one in my gym bag and one at work. For $10 a pop, why not. The Panasonic ErgoFits do not sound like they're only $10, though; the sound quality is on par with earbuds at least 5x the price. Bass is hearty, treble and mids are decently clear. The earbuds are light but not necessarily flimsy; the cords are a bit thin compared to some, but they would rip unless mishandled. The shape dictates that they sit comfortably in your ear while still providing sound isolation with the soft tips. Both my pairs have been in regular use for over 3 years, and the only wear I can see is one of my ear tips as ripped slightly, which is probably my own fault for handling them roughly at some point. Buy 3, buy 4 pairs of these, it doesn't matter because they are so cheap and blow every other earbud in this price category out of the water. You'll be astounded by the quality of sound and the comfortable fit they provide.

I ordered the SkyScout on August 16, and it was delivered today, two weeks later. In minutes, I was pointing it at the bright stars such as Vega, and listening to interesting facts and anecdotes about them. This is just what I was hoping for. The less prominent stars have no sound, but either text descriptions, or bare-bones descriptions with name, magnitude and some other statistics. Not bad. Once, when reviewing a text description of a star, the unit turned itself off, which was a little inconvenient because it takes about a minute for the GPS to reacquire the satellites. I'm fairly certain I didn't hit the power button, but I suppose that's a possibility. Another time, the unit froze with a text description on the screen and the backlight on. None of the buttons responded, and I ended up removing the batteries to reset it. A message appeared periodically complaining about magnetic interference, and recommending I move away from cars and electrical devices, even though, I was outside about 20 feet from the closest car or electrical device. Despite the messages, the magnetic compass seemed to work fine, as it correctly identified the stars I pointed at. Overall it was a mixed experience. When it worked, it was a load of fun. Waiting for the restarts after the crashes dampened my enthusiasm. I hope, I can find a way to avoid the crashes. I have not read the manual, nor have I spent much time with the unit, so these problems might be my own fault.

I've had the F2-410 (not this model but similar) for almost 3 years now and it's been a solid machine but not without issues. When I first got it I used it for file storage, backup storage, and Plex. I quickly outgrew the Plex usage. One Plex update and Plex no longer worked without buffering every 30 seconds due to CPU bottleneck. From then on I used it as file and backup storage in a RAID 1 (mirrored) configuration until I removed a drive, formatted it, and re-added it to the RAID only to have it wipe both drives. After that I pooled the drives and used it for file and backup storage. After an update, folder permissions had to be reapplied with every reboot or Windows backup would be denied access, which was annoying but manageable. With the latest software update, Windows system image backup files are all corrupt and unreadable even for new images. However, third party backup tools still work as do all shared folders. So while it's been a great piece of hardware the software leaves much to be desired and has even broken features over time. Still, it's been reliable, it just requires some concessions and workarounds. Be prepared for a learning curve and to do things a different way than you want or are used to.

The wires seem to be the standard size for breadboards. However, they're much more bendy than the wires you might get in a jumper kit from a proper electronics distributor. You get one insertion out of the wire and that's pretty much it for that part. In our lab we find we can reuse the proper kits' wires several times before retiring. Given the much shorter life, the lower cost of these doesn't make economic sense. I doubt I'll purchase these again. That said, if you're a hobbyist and want a cheaper kit for your limited use, this might be a good purchase for you.

This BE850G2 made a loud POP sound followed by the burnt electronics smell (and flipped a home circuit breaker) Plus customers expect that a higher VA rated model will have more reserve time than a lower rated model. APC knows this but uses the same battery (RBC17) in the 850BEG2 and its 650BEG1. How long a UPS powers your devices is determined by the Ah capacity of the battery, not by the VA number marketing tacks on. Plus APC will not even list the Ah rating of these models -- the most important rating.

When I first received it, in May of 2017, the dock charged both my phone and extra battery perfectly and quick (if both were at 0% it takes about 45mins-1hr). Over the last few weeks its had some trouble charging both at once, can only charge one or other at a time or takes 3+hrs to charge. This morning, as both phone and battery were charging, I awoke to the smell of burning plastic (neither had been connected longer than 2 hours). After searching my home for the source of the smell I found that, right above the rear usb port on the dock, the plastic was melting and the entire base was overheating (possible electrical fire hazard). Have not yet attempted to use again. Outside of this safety issue, would have been worth 5 stars (I'd like to mention it never got wet, dropped, or damaged prior to this) ::update:: when plugged back in will, sorta, charge phone. Extra battery charger does not work at all, and dock continues to overheat/smell of burning plastic (maybe a short somewhere). No replacement option from vender, only refund. Use at own risk. **Update** Anoke replaced defective unit at no cost, after noting the issue for review with their manufacturer. Have been using replacement dock with no issue for two months. 5 star product.

Kindle Fire 7" Second Generation As others have stated, no power adapter included with the Kindle Fire Gen 2. I bought an adapter and used an existing USB cable. I tried charging via USB cable with my computer. After several hours the charge percentage increased by 10%. When I charged it via power adapter, it wasn't much better. My battery ran down to 16% so I put it on the charger (power adapter) one day last week. It sat there for 24 hours. The charge was then at 29%. I thought, "Wow, so maybe if I leave it plugged in for a week and spend how much extra via my electric bill, the Kindle will charge 100% so I can use it for a few hours." I was spending more time charging my Kindle than using it. Amazon staff, upon hearing of this experience, went above and beyond to help me remedy this problem. I now have a different USB cable and am using the (Amazon Recommended) Kindle PowerFast adapter. Today, I turned my Kindle on for the first time in a week (I just simply stopped using it -- because of the power issues). The battery was at 16%. I plugged in the new USB cable and adapter and figured I'd check it in a few hours to see if it had gone any higher. I expected it to be at about 40% or so. To my surprise, it was at 100%. That's the first time I've seen the battery at 100% in WEEKS. OMGosh -- THANK YOU, AMAZON! :)

This kit is pretty nice and helped me hide wiring that goes up along a wall to my projector, as well as some speaker and sub woofer wiring around a door frame. The included 3 pieces of the cord channel were just enough for my application, but just barely. One kit will probably not be enough for most applications. The pieces fit together nicely, the connectors are handy, and everything looks neat. The adhesive is fairly strong, which I found out when I tried to unstick a piece of the channel because it was flush against the door molding, and a connector wasn't fitting around it as a result. Each channel should comfortably fit 2 coax size cables, but not too much more. My biggest gripe with the product is the quality of the plastic where the channel hinges open. When I got mine, part of it was already cracked along the length of the hinge. While I was cutting another one shorter, I pressed down on the top of the channel while holding it down, and it just cracked through in that spot too. After that I was careful about how I held the pieces and avoided additional grief, but perhaps the plastic should be a bit thicker or less brittle to prevent this. Fortunately, the cracks weren't too visible and the product got the job done, so only one star lost!

Would recommend this set of speakers to all who are looking for a set that isn't out of this world expensive.

The Klipsch Image S4i- II headphones offer great sound quality. I can tell that they are good because in certain songs the stereo will take over and it sounds like some music is coming from behind me while other parts of the song will be in front of me. They cover the entire spatial range the way the artist who made the music intended. While the bass isn't thumping it is adequate on many southern rap songs that I listen to. The inline mic works well with my iPhone 5 and is easy to use but doesn't weigh down the headphones. Earbuds come in a variety of sizes and "seal-out" outside sounds which allows me to really get in the zone when working or studying and listening to music. So far so good. I was able to get these on sale at $39.99. For that price I wish I would've bought another pair as a gift. Don't rap these headphones around your phone or music player as the cord won't tangle and it will enhance the longevity of the chord.

The company YobiTech that supplied the Drives in my order where not operational as the described "450GB SAS Hitachi UltraStar 15K450 15000RPM 16MB Cache 3.5 Oem". As near as I can tell after contacting HTSG support is these came out of a Hitachi Storage system and had special firmware burned in them. They do NOT work on a standard SAS 3Gb LSI controller, nor will they accept a standard partition table. (for use in Windows or linux... they are just good for the magnets inside them). I contacted the company and they have communicated back to me that they will not make good on the product they shipped me. The company YobiTech said they had a 3 yr warranty directly on my order. But they will not fix them nor replace them. The company YobiTech said they has a 100% Satisfaction Guarantee, but they must of meant themselves and not the customer, for I am zero percent satisified. What they shipped me was electronic waste. It has taken me a long time to verify it is not my equipment, software nor installation as to the reason they do not work, and the company Yobitech will not stand up to what they were supposed to delivery. I would not trust this company on delivering what they say they will.

Timbuk2 has long been on my list of go-to vendors so I sought them out when I received my Kindle as a present. This case is constructed out of durable nylon, similar to many of their messenger bags, faux fur on the inside, and thick memory foam-like padding. IMO the color combination is subtle with just the right touch of color - although I would appreciate it more if the logo were lime rather than black. The closure is secure with three velcro tabs holding it closed. On the inside the faux fur keeps the kindle snug and protected without any worry of scratching it. The padding is ample and protects the kindle without adding bulk - in hot climates (90F +), the padding does become more supple but still provides protection. After using it for several months, I purchased it's matching sibling to protect my iPad. Highly recommended if you're looking for a kindle sleeve, and yes, I would recommend this to my friends.
